draft.introduction: Romans 8:28–39 is the climactic peroration of Paul’s argument, moving from providence (“all things work together for good”) through predestination to the triumphant declaration that nothing can separate believers from God’s love.

translation.text: And we know that for those who love God all things work together for good, for those who are called according to his purpose. For those whom he foreknew he also predestined to be conformed to the image of his Son.
translation.original_text: οἴδαμεν δὲ ὅτι τοῖς ἀγαπῶσιν τὸν θεὸν πάντα συνεργεῖ εἰς ἀγαθόν

translator_notes.note: The phrase “conformed to the image of his Son” (συμμόρφους τῆς εἰκόνος τοῦ υἱοῦ αὐτοῦ) is a key theosis text: the destiny of believers is to share in the form of Christ. Eastern theology reads this as deification; Western theology typically reads it as moral transformation.
